address: Hua Hin Beach Road, Hua Hin, Prachuap Khiri Khan
best_time_to_visit: Early morning for a peaceful walk and sunrise, or late afternoon for horse rides in golden light

description.en:
## Hua Hin's Iconic 5-Kilometre Beach

Hua Hin Beach is the resort town's main attraction and the reason the Thai royal family first chose this coastline as their seaside retreat over a century ago. The wide, sandy beach stretches roughly 5 kilometres from the town centre south to Khao Takiap (Chopstick Mountain), offering a mix of relaxation, water sports, and beachfront dining along its length.

## Horse Riding on the Sand

Hua Hin is one of the few places in Thailand where you can ride a horse along the beach. About 60 horses are available daily, with most gathered near the main beach entrance close to the Sofitel Hotel. A 20-minute ride costs around 400 THB, 30 minutes is 500 THB, and a full hour runs about 1,000 THB. It is a quintessential Hua Hin experience and makes for unforgettable photos, especially at sunset.

## Beach Activities

Beyond horse riding, the beach offers kitesurfing (particularly popular from March to May when winds pick up), kayaking, jet skiing, and swimming. Sun loungers with umbrellas can be rented from beachfront vendors for around 100–200 THB per set. The southern end near Khao Takiap is quieter and ideal for those seeking a more peaceful stretch of sand.

## Beachfront Dining

Dozens of restaurants and bars line the shore, ranging from simple seafood shacks serving grilled prawns and cold beer to upscale hotel restaurants. Watching the sunset with fresh seafood and a coconut shake in hand is the classic Hua Hin evening experience.

## Getting There

The beach runs parallel to the town centre and is accessible from multiple sois (side streets) off Phetkasem Road. Most hotels in Hua Hin are within walking distance.
description.nl: Hua Hin's hoofdstrand dat zich kilometers uitstrekt met wit zand, paardritten en dineren aan het strand

tips: Negotiate horse riding prices before mounting — agree on the duration and route in advance
The northern end near the Hilton is best for swimming with calmer water and lifeguards on duty
Bring sunscreen and reapply frequently — the sea breeze disguises how strong the sun actually is
